related.title: Andy Burnham to cut VAT on electricity bills as first cost-of-living move
related.description: Newly appointed chancellor John Healey said move would be funded from cancelling digital ID programme Electricity bills will be reduced by an average of £45 from October, after Andy Burnham announced the government would remove VAT from them. related.title: Wealth tax on UK’s super-rich could raise £10bn a year, Andy Burnham told
related.description: New prime minister urged to introduce 2% minimum charge on households with more than £100m in wealth A wealth tax on the UK’s super-rich households could raise £10bn a year, according to academics who urged Andy Burnham to include the measure in his plans to “make tax fairer” and pay for better public services. related.title: Andy Burnham vows to ‘bring back hope’ as he unveils his cabinet
related.description: New prime minister says government must ‘do better’ to deliver change but promises prudent approach to economy Andy Burnham has said his arrival in power will “bring back hope” to Britain as he launched his premiership with the surprise appointment of John Healey as chancellor and a promise to repair the long-term damage caused to communities during the Thatcher era. articles.title: UK employers cut job vacancies as Burnham aims to lift living standards
articles.description: Unemployment holds steady at 4.9%, underlining the challenge facing the new prime minister Business live – latest updates Employers cut the number of job vacancies in May, according to official figures that highlight the UK’s “fragile” economic outlook amid the conflict in the Middle East. Job vacancies fell to 712,000 – almost half the level in 2022 – as employers put off hiring new staff in the three months to May, the Office for National Statistics said. Continue reading...

articles.title: Gaza celebrates World Cup victory of Spanish team they took into their hearts
articles.description: People gather at cafes and outdoor screenings to support team after Spain’s backing for Palestinian rights The Spanish football team’s World Cup win has been celebrated in the streets of Gaza, where Spain’s support for Palestinian rights and Israel’s backing for the Argentinian team turned the competition into more than a football showdown. Gathered at cafes and outdoor screenings, Palestinians defied the risk of Israeli attacks to cheer on a team they had taken to their hearts. Continue reading

articles.title: British Gas mixed up our energy meter – but now I’m being penalised
articles.description: When the problem was finally sorted out in our new-build home, it increased my tariff by 19% Residents in our new housing development are being billed for each other’s energy after the meters were mixed up. articles.description: Experts urge ministers to update legislation to prevent intended parents resorting to riskier arrangements abroad Surrogates, parents and children are being failed by the UK’s archaic laws, which are “creaking at the seams” as demand for surrogacy grows rapidly, experts have said. articles.title: Gruel and graft: museum asks if you could survive a Victorian workhouse

articles.title: José Pizarro’s recipe for tomato and bread salad with boquerones
articles.description: Spanish white pickled anchovies top this full-flavoured dish that’s easy to make and made for sharing In Spain, some of our most loved dishes come from making something special out of very little. This salad is exactly that kind of food: it’s about using what is already in the house and turning it into something generous and full-flavoured. articles.title: Not enough water for UK’s datacentre plans, trade body says
articles.description: Industry says government’s failure to address cooling demands means AI growth plans are ‘fatally flawed’ The UK will not have enough water for future datacentres, the water industry has said in stark criticism of the government’s AI growth plans. articles.title: Richard Dadd review – can his mental illness help explain these arresting, bizarre images?
articles.description: Royal Academy, LondonDadd was already a remarkable artist – with fairies as his hit theme – before he was locked up for murder. Yet it was within the confines of Bedlam that he saw nature and art afresh The young man in the garden must be one of Richard Dadd’s fellow patients in Bedlam, you think. His unkempt hair and ruffled clothes are sure indications of a distracted mind as he sits, half his face in shadow, hands nervously clasped, on a fabulously twisted wooden bench surrounded by greenery
